item.title: Doctor checks for OpenClaw v0.8.6+ upgrade issues — migration state, tool access grants, underscore tool methods
item.intent: clawhq doctor must detect upgrade-related issues introduced by OpenClaw v0.8.6–v0.8.10 so users upgrading existing agents don't hit silent failures.
item.description: Three new doctor checks needed: (1) Post-upgrade migration verification — verify database migrations completed (they previously failed silently). (2) Tool access grants — detect tools without explicit access grants and warn that they are admin-only on v0.8.7+ (related to BUG-114 but doctor check is independently useful for existing agents). (3) Underscore tool methods — detect tools with underscore-prefixed public methods that are no longer exposed to the LLM on v0.8.10+. CRITICAL PREREQUISITE: No OpenClaw version detection mechanism exists anywhere in the codebase. Version-gating requires implementing a version query first (options: docker exec to read package.json, parse image tag from docker-compose, or add Gateway RPC version endpoint). Without this, checks must either run unconditionally or this item is blocked.
steps: Implement OpenClaw version detection — determine running version via docker exec, image tag parsing, or Gateway RPC (no mechanism currently exists)
Add version detection to doctor infrastructure (src/operate/doctor/) so checks can query it
Add check: database migration verification (run unconditionally — safe on all versions)
Add check: tool access grants missing (version-gate to v0.8.7+ if version detection works, otherwise run unconditionally with note in output)
Add check: underscore tool methods (version-gate to v0.8.10+ if version detection works, otherwise run unconditionally with note in output)
Coordinate with BUG-114 AC3 — reuse the same tool access grant check logic, don't duplicate
Add tests for all 3 checks and version-gating logic
Run npm test to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq doctor checks that database migrations completed successfully
clawhq doctor warns if any tool server connections lack explicit access grants
clawhq doctor warns if any installed tools have underscore-prefixed methods that were previously LLM-visible
Checks are version-gated when OpenClaw version is detectable; run unconditionally with advisory note when version is unknown
Existing doctor tests pass with no regression
